question_text: A student stretches a spring with a force of 12 N and measures an extension of 0.3 m. Calculate the spring constant of the spring. (Use F = ke)
explanation: Using F = ke, rearrange to find k = F/e. Therefore k = 12 N / 0.3 m = 40 N/m. The spring constant tells us that 40 newtons of force would stretch this spring by 1 metre.
